steps.title: Spot the divergent paths
steps.content: The passage emphasizes the couple's “divergent experiences”—the signal that we're comparing two parallel but different journeys. After Ifemelu's path, we need a transition showing Obinze's path happens at the same time but in a different direction.

steps.title: Choose the transition for simultaneity
steps.content:
“Meanwhile” shows Obinze's journey happening at the same time as Ifemelu's—the right expression for two simultaneous but separate paths.

The other three misrepresent this: “Nevertheless” implies they contradict, “Secondly” treats them as sequential items, and “In fact” suggests emphasis rather than parallelism.

That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
